mechanism_narrative: When CPI inflation is running well above target while the federal funds rate is rising, equity discount rates reprice upward faster than near-term earnings, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 288.663 and FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.33 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A sub-4% unemployment rate combined with continued payroll gains indicates late-cycle labor tightness that raises wage pressure and policy-tightening risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 3.6 and PAYEMS latest snapshot value 151314.0 as of the decision date
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with tight labor markets
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations do not underperform the unconditional 60-day return by at least 50 basis points on average in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: An upward trend in retail sales signals resilient household demand and stronger nominal revenue growth for index constituents, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 667724.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of the 3-month RSAFS trend has nonpositive Rank IC with 20-day S&P 500 returns over two consecutive 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rising industrial production trend reflects expanding real output and operating leverage, causing analysts to revise earnings expectations upward and generating positive S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 104.5853 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 6-month trends are followed by lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative INDPRO trends in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp downturn in housing starts signals weakening interest-rate-sensitive demand and future construction-sector earnings pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST 3-month change < -10% or HOUST crosses below its 12-month moving average
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 1793.0 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST downturn triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly increase in the federal funds rate above 20 basis points represents an abrupt tightening impulse that raises short-horizon discount rates and liquidity premia, causing negative S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 0.33 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered FEDFUNDS increases are followed by nonnegative average 5-day S&P 500 returns after transaction-date alignment in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: When unemployment is below 4% and CPI momentum is falling, strong labor income supports consumption without adding policy-tightening pressure, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE < 4.0 and CPIAUCSL 3-month annualized change < CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized change
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 3.6 and CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 288.663 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if this joint condition produces a 20-day forward hit rate below 50% or a mean return below the unconditional 20-day mean over a full walk-forward sample.

mechanism_narrative: Negative real GDP growth combined with high CPI inflation indicates stagflation risk, compressing equity multiples and earnings expectations and causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 latest quarter-over-quarter change < 0 and CPIAUCSL 12-month change > 4%
evidence_refs: fact:GDPC1 latest snapshot value 19735.895 and CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 288.663 as of the decision date
failure_condition: Reject if triggered stagflation observations do not have lower average 60-day S&P 500 returns than observations with positive GDPC1 growth and CPIAUCSL 12-month change below 4%.
